focus: RQ4 deepening — permissions-matrix schema + RM-8 walkthrough

summary: Full permissions-matrix.schema.json definition with fields {version, default_action, scope_tokens, rules, validation_rules} and example matrix entries for read-only-corpus, packet-local-write, and repo-wide-write patterns
acceptance_criteria: Schema validates against JSON Schema draft-07, example entries demonstrate three permission patterns (read-only-corpus, packet-local-write, repo-wide-write), scope_tokens support {repo-root}, {packet-root}, {state-paths.*} substitution

summary: RM-8 counter-example walkthrough confirms permissions-matrix schema would have prevented all 44 file deletions by denying Write operations on spec docs and review/ subtrees in read-only-corpus scope, and denying Exec operations (including rm commands) entirely in read-only-corpus scope
acceptance_criteria: Schema rules #7 (Write deny in read-only-corpus) and #8 (Exec deny in read-only-corpus) block all RM-8 deletion patterns, pre-tool-call hook enforces decisions before execution independent of --dangerously-skip-permissions flag

summary: Schema location verdict: .opencode/skills/cli-opencode/assets/permissions-matrix.schema.json based on iter-005 HYBRID approach (distributed references/ files + enhances edges, no new skill) and cli-devin agent-config recipe pattern precedent
acceptance_criteria: Schema co-located with cli-opencode execution layer for tight integration, enables skill-specific customization, avoids new shared skill per iter-005 HYBRID verdict

summary: Runtime enforcement design: pre-tool-call hook in cli-opencode execution layer loads schema, matches tool calls against rules, rejects violations before execution independent of --dangerously-skip-permissions flag, with optional post-tool-call audit hook for defense-in-depth
acceptance_criteria: Pre-tool-call hook enforces allow/deny decisions before tool execution, replaces Layer 1 (prose-based prompt hardening) while retaining Layers 2-4 (worktree isolation, commit-before-dispatch, model selection), logs scope_violation events to JSONL
